evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my iOS app. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.swift. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration code into EmptyViewController.swift (DataCaptureContext.initialize + .shared, BarcodeCaptureSettings, BarcodeCapture(context:settings:), BarcodeCaptureListener, Camera.default + BarcodeCapture.recommendedCameraSettings, DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, viewWillAppear/viewWillDisappear lifecycle), and shows the setup checklist (SPM package, NSCameraUsageDescription, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding ScanditBarcodeCapture and ScanditCaptureCore via Swift Package Manager (datacapture-spm)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.initialize(licenseKey: is used (v7 API, not the deprecated constructor)
DataCaptureContext(licenseKey: is NOT present in the new code
DataCaptureContext.shared is read after initialize
BarcodeCapture.recommendedCameraSettings is used (no parentheses — it is a property)
Camera.default is used to obtain the camera
context.setFrameSource( is called with the camera
BarcodeCaptureSettings is constructed and configured
Symbology .ean13UPCA is enabled
Symbology .code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than ean13UPCA and code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) is used to create the mode
barcodeCapture.addListener( is called
DataCaptureView(context:frame:) is used to create the preview
BarcodeCaptureOverlay(barcodeCapture:view:) is called
The view controller (or an extension of it) conforms to BarcodeCaptureListener
barcodeCapture(_:didScanIn:frameData:) is implemented and reads session.newlyRecognizedBarcode
DispatchQueue.main.async is used inside the didScanIn callback for UI work
camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .on) is called in viewWillAppear (or equivalent lifecycle hook)
camera?.switch(toDesiredState: .off) is called in viewWillDisappear (or equivalent lifecycle hook)
context.removeCurrentMode() is called in deinit (or equivalent cleanup hook)

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing iOS view controller. Here it is: EmptyViewController.swift. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, print the barcode data to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into EmptyViewController.swift with QR and Data Matrix symbologies enabled. didScanIn prints the barcode data using print(). Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology .qr is enabled
Symbology .dataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than qr and dataMatrix are enabled
BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) is used
barcodeCapture(_:didScanIn:frameData:) is implemented and accesses barcode.data
The scan result is logged (print or os_log)
viewWillAppear turns the camera on
viewWillDisappear turns the camera off
Setup checklist mentions Swift Package Manager / datacapture-spm

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my iOS BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (an async function call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false at the start of the didScanIn callback, launches a Task for the async lookup, and re-enables scanning when the Task completes (e.g. in a defer block).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.isEnabled = false is set inside didScanIn before the async work
A Task { } or async completion handler is used to perform the async lookup
barcodeCapture.isEnabled = true is set after the lookup completes (defer or final step)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my iOS app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns barcodeCapture.feedback.success to a Feedback constructed with vibration: nil and sound: nil.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.feedback.success is reassigned to a Feedback with no sound and no vibration (vibration: nil, sound: nil)
BarcodeCaptureFeedback() empty constructor is NOT used (that is the Android API)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my iOS BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim. Update IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with .square style and assigns it to overlay.viewfinder.

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with style: .square
The viewfinder is assigned to a BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property (overlay.viewfinder = ...)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets codeDuplicateFilter = 0.5 (TimeInterval, seconds) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance before creating the BarcodeCapture mode (or applies new settings via barcodeCapture.apply).

assertions.text: codeDuplicateFilter is set to 0.5 (seconds) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings — NOT 500 and NOT TimeInterval.millis(500)
codeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture(context:settings:) is invoked, or via barcodeCapture.apply
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add an aimer viewfinder to my iOS BarcodeCapture preview so users have a target dot to aim at. Update IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs an AimerViewfinder and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property.

assertions.text: An AimerViewfinder() is constructed
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a horizontal laser line viewfinder on my iOS BarcodeCapture preview to guide users when scanning long 1D codes. Update IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs a LaserlineViewfinder and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay's viewfinder property.

assertions.text: A LaserlineViewfinder() is constructed
The viewfinder is assigned to the overlay via overlay.viewfinder =
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Restrict barcode recognition to a rectangle in the center of the preview so codes outside it are ignored. Use a relative size of 90% width and 30% height. Update my BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a SizeWithUnit using FloatWithUnit values with MeasureUnit.fraction (0.9 width, 0.3 height), creates a RectangularLocationSelection(size:), and assigns it to settings.locationSelection.

assertions.text: A FloatWithUnit(value: 0.9, unit: .fraction) is constructed for the width
A FloatWithUnit(value: 0.3, unit: .fraction) is constructed for the height
A SizeWithUnit(width:height:) is constructed from the FloatWithUnit values
A RectangularLocationSelection(size:) is constructed
settings.locationSelection is assigned the RectangularLocationSelection
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I only want to recognize barcodes near the center of the screen, inside a circular area with a radius of 20% of the view width. Configure this on my BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill builds a FloatWithUnit radius with MeasureUnit.fraction (0.2), creates a RadiusLocationSelection(radius:), and assigns it to settings.locationSelection.

assertions.text: A FloatWithUnit(value: 0.2, unit: .fraction) is constructed for the radius
A RadiusLocationSelection(radius:) is constructed
settings.locationSelection is assigned the RadiusLocationSelection
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my iOS BarcodeCapture app I only want to accept barcodes whose data starts with the prefix "978" (ISBNs). Other codes should be ignored and not highlighted. Wire this rejection logic into IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill, inside barcodeCapture(_:didScanIn:frameData:), reads barcode.data and checks barcode.data.hasPrefix("978"); when it does not match it sets the overlay brush to a transparent Brush (Brush.transparent) and returns without handling the scan.

assertions.text: Inside didScanIn the code reads barcode.data
The barcode data is checked with .hasPrefix("978")
When the prefix does not match, overlay.brush is set to Brush.transparent (or a Brush with transparent fill and stroke)
(semantic) the callback returns early for rejected codes so non-matching barcodes are not handled
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Change the color of the highlight drawn around recognized barcodes in my iOS BarcodeCapture app to a semi-transparent green fill with a solid green stroke. Update IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs a Brush(fill:stroke:strokeWidth:) with green colors and assigns it to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay via overlay.brush.

assertions.text: A Brush(fill:stroke:strokeWidth:) is constructed
The brush is assigned to the overlay via overlay.brush =
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I scan Code 39 barcodes that contain full ASCII characters in my iOS app. Enable the full ASCII extension for Code 39 in my BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains the Code 39 symbology settings via settings.settings(for: .code39) and enables the extension with set(extension: "full_ascii", enabled: true).

assertions.text: settings.settings(for: .code39) is used to obtain the SymbologySettings
set(extension: "full_ascii", enabled: true) is called on the symbology settings
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My Code 39 barcodes use a mod 43 checksum. Configure my iOS BarcodeCapture settings to validate that checksum in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains the Code 39 symbology settings via settings.settings(for: .code39) and sets symbologySettings.checksums = [.mod43].

assertions.text: settings.settings(for: .code39) is used to obtain the SymbologySettings
checksums is set to [.mod43] on the symbology settings
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: My Code 128 barcodes vary in length between 7 and 20 characters. Configure the active symbol counts for Code 128 in my iOS BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains the Code 128 symbology settings via settings.settings(for: .code128) and sets symbologySettings.activeSymbolCounts = Set(7...20).

assertions.text: settings.settings(for: .code128) is used to obtain the SymbologySettings
activeSymbolCounts is set to Set(7...20) on the symbology settings
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: Some of my QR codes are printed light-on-dark (color inverted). Enable scanning of color-inverted QR codes in my iOS BarcodeCapture settings in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill obtains the QR symbology settings via settings.settings(for: .qr) and sets symbologySettings.isColorInvertedEnabled = true.

assertions.text: settings.settings(for: .qr) is used to obtain the SymbologySettings
isColorInvertedEnabled is set to true on the symbology settings
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan GS1 Composite Code type A and type B (a 2D component on top of a 1D code) in my iOS BarcodeCapture app. Configure my BarcodeCapture settings to support these composite codes in IntegratedViewController.swift.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.enabledCompositeTypes to include .a and .b, and calls settings.enableSymbologies(forCompositeTypes:) with the same composite types to enable the underlying symbologies.

assertions.text: settings.enabledCompositeTypes is set to include CompositeType .a and .b
settings.enableSymbologies(forCompositeTypes:) is called with the composite types
Existing integration code (context, barcodeCapture, camera, listener) is preserved
